hypotheses.claim: The manuscript's text structure reflects a mix of prose and verse, with the herbal section being primarily composed of short, rhyming couplets.
evidence_for: The herbal section has higher entropy levels compared to other sections
The word-final glyph distribution in the herbal section shows a higher frequency of 'y' and 'n', which are common in rhyming couplets
hypotheses.test: Run a chi-squared test on the word-final glyph distribution in the herbal section compared to other sections
tests_remaining: Run a chi-squared test on the word-final glyph distribution in the herbal section compared to other sections
hypotheses.reasoning: If confirmed, this would explain the unique characteristics of the herbal section and provide insight into the manuscript's text structure

hypotheses.claim: The Currier A/B split reflects a genuine linguistic difference between two distinct languages or dialects.
evidence_for: The Currier Language A and B splits show significant differences in vocabulary and syntax
hypotheses.test: Run a Kullback-Leibler divergence test on the language models of Currier Language A and B
tests_remaining: Run a Kullback-Leibler divergence test on the language models of Currier Language A and B
hypotheses.reasoning: If confirmed, this would provide strong evidence for a linguistic difference between two distinct languages or dialects

hypotheses.claim: The text uses a combination of substitution and transposition ciphers in the biological section.
evidence_for: The biological section has higher entropy levels compared to other sections
The word-final glyph distribution in the biological section shows a higher frequency of 'l' and 'r', which are common in transposition ciphers
hypotheses.test: Run a mutual information test on the relationship between glyphs in the biological section
tests_remaining: Run a mutual information test on the relationship between glyphs in the biological section
hypotheses.reasoning: If confirmed, this would provide insight into the cipher structure used in the manuscript

hypotheses.claim: The text is encoded using a simple substitution cipher on an unknown language.
evidence_for: The glyph entropy levels are lower than expected for Latin or Italian
The word-final glyph distribution shows no clear pattern matching known languages
hypotheses.test: Run a language identification test on the text using machine learning algorithms
tests_remaining: Run a language identification test on the text using machine learning algorithms
hypotheses.reasoning: If confirmed, this would provide evidence for an unknown language and narrow the solution space

hypotheses.claim: The text uses a combination of substitution and transposition ciphers in the herbal section.
evidence_for: The herbal section has higher entropy levels compared to other sections
The word-final glyph distribution in the herbal section shows a higher frequency of 'y' and 'n', which are common in transposition ciphers
hypotheses.test: Run a mutual information test on the relationship between glyphs in the herbal section
tests_remaining: Run a mutual information test on the relationship between glyphs in the herbal section
hypotheses.reasoning: If confirmed, this would provide insight into the cipher structure used in the manuscript
